channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Liverpool sack Arne Slot after disastrous Premier League title defence
item.description: Liverpool have sacked head coach Arne Slot after a fifth-place finish in their Premier League title defence.

item.title: Confirmed Ebola cases nearly double in days as WHO chief visits DR Congo
item.description: WHO's Tedros calls for a community-led fight as a rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly through conflict-hit eastern DRC.

item.title: ‘Opposite visions’: What to know about Colombia’s presidential election
item.description: Senator Ivan Cepeda is leading two right-wing rivals in the first round of an election dominated by security concerns.

item.title: Indonesia’s Mount Merapi volcano erupts, spewing ash into the sky
item.description: Videos show Indonesia’s Mount Merapi spewing a column of ash around 2 kilometres high in West Sumatra.

item.title: Senior Sri Lankan monk suspended over child sex abuse allegation
item.description: Pallegama Hemarathana is accused of abusing an 11-year-old girl in a Buddhist temple in 2022.

item.title: Rescuers free four more men from flooded Laos cave, two still missing
item.description: Five of seven men who entered cave seeking gold are now out after being trapped for 10 days.

item.title: Truck carrying Afghan returnees from Pakistan flips on highway, killing 18
item.description: At least 10 children among the dead as a packed truck carrying families returning from Pakistan flips on major highway.

item.title: Israeli soldiers reach Nabatieh, one of southern Lebanon’s biggest cities
item.description: Israel’s military has advanced beyond Lebanon's Litani River for the first time since 2006.

item.title: Violence against foreign-owned shops in South Africa
item.description: Foreign workers in South Africa are again facing violence and protests by anti-immigrant groups.

item.title: WHO chief visits Bunia, epicentre of the Ebola outbreak
item.description: WHO Chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is visiting Bunia in the DRC, the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: The Iran war is dividing Muslims in the Philippines
item.description: Divisive foreign political narratives are being wrongly cast as religious discourse within our Muslim communities.

item.title: Cuba Calls Guantanamo Talks with U.S. General ‘Positive’
item.description: Senior U.S. and Cuban military officials held a rare face-to-face meeting near Guantanamo Bay.

item.title: Egypt warns Israel that dangerous Gaza escalations threaten ceasefire
item.description: Egypt races to salvage Gaza ceasefire as Israeli attacks and displacement threats push deal to the brink of collapse.

item.title: LIVE: PSG vs Arsenal – Champions League final
item.description: Follow our live build-up, with full team news coverage, ahead of our text commentary stream of UEFA's showpiece final.

item.title: Liverpool sack Arne Slot after disastrous Premier League title defence
item.description: The Dutchman won the Premier League title in his first season but Liverpool finished fifth in the 2025/26 season.

item.title: North Korean POWs: Political pawns in Russia’s war on Ukraine?
item.description: 101 East investigates the plight of two North Korean POWs in Ukraine and the campaign to transfer them to South Korea.

item.title: What Hegseth’s comments at Shangri-La Dialogue say about US foreign policy
item.description: The US has addressed defence leaders in Singapore, outlining America's geopolitical goals.

item.title: Israel launches more than 10 strikes across southern Lebanon
item.description: Videos show the aftermath of renewed Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon that killed at least three people.

item.title: Rubio says Trump envoy Barrack to step down from Syria post
item.description: Trump envoy Tom Barrack to exit formal Syria post but retain key role managing US policy in Syria and Iraq.

item.title: Damage seen in northern Israel after Hezbollah launches rocket barrages
item.description: Israel says it has intercepted an air attack from Hezbollah on Saturday, May 30th.

item.title: Vallejo fined for sexist remarks about female referee at French Open
item.description: Paraguayan blames Brazilian referee Ana Carvalho for failing to control the home crowd during his second-round loss.

item.title: Police fire tear gas during protest outside New Jersey ICE facility
item.description: Police fired tear gas and used horses to push back protesters outside the Delaney Hall immigration detention facility.

item.title: Officer accuses disabled driver of holding phone with missing hand
item.description: A traffic stop in Florida has gone viral after an officer accused a disabled driver of holding a phone in her right hand

item.title: Ukraine drones strike Russian oil facility, as Kyiv and Moscow trade blows
item.description: Strikes have seen 13,000 residents without power in Zaporizhzhia as Russia and Ukraine trade drone attacks.

item.title: Voting ongoing in snap elections in Malta, governing party expected to win
item.description: Malta's economy to dominate this year’s election, with rising rents and infrastructure at the forefront of debates.
